behavior_in_task_description.reason: All file paths (packages/react-router/src/Match.tsx, packages/solid-router/src/Match.tsx, packages/solid-router/src/not-found.tsx, packages/vue-router/src/Match.tsx), function names (getNotFound), and literal patterns (error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId, notFoundError.routeId ??=, etc.) checked in test assertions are explicitly mentioned in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md specifies the exact fix: 'error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId' appearing twice in React, 'getNotFound' function with specific cause-checking logic in Solid, and exact patterns like 'error.routeId ??= matchData.value?.routeId as any' for Vue. This tells the agent exactly WHAT to change, not just what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests like test_react_match_assigns_routeId_onCatch assert exact string 'error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId', test_solid_notFound_exports_getNotFound checks for 'export function getNotFound' and '(error as any)?.cause', test_vue_match_assigns_routeId checks 'error.routeId ??= matchData.value?.routeId as any'. An alternative correct fix using different variable names, helper patterns, or assignment approaches would fail these text-matching tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_react_match_assigns_routeId_onCatch, test_react_match_assigns_routeId_in_fallback, test_solid_match_uses_getNotFound_helper, test_solid_notFound_exports_getNotFound, test_vue_match_assigns_routeId) are all string-in-file checks using _file_contains. They do 'assert content_check' where content_check = _file_contains(filepath, "error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId"). While pass-to-pass tests run actual test suites, the core f2p tests are pure text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests check for string patterns in files (e.g., test_react_match_assigns_routeId_onCatch checks if 'error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId' exists in Match.tsx). An agent could simply append a dead-code comment containing the exact string to each file without implementing any actual fix, and the f2p tests would pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors described in instruction.md are tested: React routeId assignment in onCatch and fallback, Solid getNotFound helper with cause checking, Solid Match.tsx integration, Vue routeId assignment in both handlers, plus pass-to-pass tests for unit tests, types, and eslint.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are clearly grouped with comments ('Fail-to-pass tests' and 'Pass-to-pass tests'), well-named (test_react_match_assigns_routeId_onCatch, test_solid_notFound_exports_getNotFound, etc.), and include docstrings explaining what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves code fixes, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, and commands in instruction.md and tests are consistent and correct.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md specifies exact file paths AND exact code patterns to insert: 'error.routeId ??= matchState.routeId appearing twice', 'error.routeId ??= matchData.value?.routeId as any', 'export function getNotFound', '(error as any)?.cause'. This goes far beyond identifying buggy files and gives the exact implementation.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agentmd-edits config task.
